title: lichen_l2_iface_init sets lichen_iface before checking all init steps can succeed
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:530-563

**What goes wrong:** The initialization sequence is:
1. Init LoRa driver (line 492-497) - can fail, sets iface_init_failed
2. Get EUI-64 (line 500-505) - can fail, sets iface_init_failed
3. Set link addr (line 513-516)
4. Init link_ctx (line 520-527)
5. Set lichen_iface = iface (line 530)
6. Register RX callback (line 533)
7. Log link-local (line 536-540) - can fail, goes to fail_late_init

The issue: lichen_iface is set at line 530 but lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 can fail at line 536. If it fails, the code goes to fail_late_init which clears the callback but does NOT clear lichen_iface (due to "set-once invariant"). However, iface_init_failed IS set.

The lora_rx_callback checks both lichen_iface == NULL and iface_init_failed, so this is actually handled correctly. Not a bug.

Retracting.
close_reason: Not a bug - lora_rx_callback checks both lichen_iface AND iface_init_failed, so late init failure is properly handled
